segments.text: I said, 'I will watch my ways and keep my tongue from sin; I will put a muzzle on my mouth while in the presence of the wicked.'
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: my mouth…: Heb. a bridle, or, muzzle for my mouth

segments.text: I held my peace to no avail, and my distress grew worse.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: My heart grew hot within me; while I meditated, the fire burned. Then I spoke with my tongue:
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: 'O Lord, make me know my end and what is the measure of my days; let me know how fleeting I am!'
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: how…: or, what time I have here

segments.text: Behold, you have made my days a few handbreadths, and my lifetime is as nothing before you. Surely all mankind stands as a mere breath!
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Surely a man goes about as a mere shadow; surely in vain he bustles about; he heaps up wealth and does not know who will gather!
'And now, O Lord, for what do I wait? My hope is in you.'
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Deliver me from all my transgressions. Do not make me the scorn of the fool!
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: I am mute; I do not open my mouth, for it is you who have done it.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Remove your stroke from me; I am overwhelmed by the conflict of your hand.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: When you discipline a man with rebukes for sin, you consume his desire like a moth. Surely all mankind is a mere breath!

footnotes: his…: Heb. that which is to be desired in him to melt away

segments.text: Hear my prayer, O Lord, and give ear to my cry; hold not your peace at my tears! For I am a sojourner with you, a guest, like all my fathers.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Look away from me, that I may smile again, before I depart and am no more!
segments.speaker: David
